description_long: This guard stone is a cylindrical, carved object made from a reddish-brown stone or wood, featuring a prominent human face with a long beard and stern expression on its upper section. The head is topped with a rounded cap adorned with geometric patterns, while the body is decorated with horizontal bands of carvings, including runic inscriptions and a circular symbol resembling a swastika. The surface is textured, with visible tool marks and a matte finish, suggesting it was hand-carved. The back of the object is less detailed, with the carvings concentrated on the front and sides. The overall appearance is reminiscent of Slavic or Norse totemic figures, often used as protective markers or boundary stones in historical urban or rural settings.
description: A cylindrical, hand-carved guard stone with a stern human face, runic inscriptions, and geometric patterns, made from reddish-brown stone or wood. The front is highly detailed, while the back is plain and textured.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed human face, beard, and most of the runic and geometric carvings, including the circular symbol.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the profile of the face and the depth of the carvings, with the cylindrical shape and some side patterns visible.
description_view_2: The rear view is mostly plain, showing the cylindrical form with minimal decoration and a small notch or hole in the upper section.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the side of the face and beard, with some geometric patterns and the overall texture of the carved surface.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, historical sites, or along sidewalks in urban or park settings, often marking boundaries or providing protection near pedestrian pathways.
attribute_installation_orientation: Intended to be installed fully above ground, standing upright and possibly anchored into a solid base for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on the edges and protruding carvings due to occasional contact with vehicles or pedestrians, as well as surface discoloration and erosion from weather exposure, especially on the upper and front-facing areas.
attribute_historical_markings: Features runic inscriptions and a circular symbol resembling a swastika on the front, with geometric patterns on the cap and body, all carved in a traditional style.
